title: adonis aestivalis
intro: Adonis aestivalis, also known as the pheasant's eye, is a flowering plant species within the buttercup family. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Adonis aestivalis include its ability to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antioxidant and to improve circulation. Herbal preparations of Adonis aestivalis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from the plant's roots, leaves, or flowers.
